content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


June 19, 1987


George E. Cantu, Trustee
Richmond Unified School District
1108 Bissell Avenue
P.O. Box 4014
Richmond, CA 94804                                    87-186

Dear Mr. Cantu:

We have received a copy of your letter to Ms. Eddis
Harrison, President of the Board of Trustees of the Richmond
Unified School District, concerning your disqualification from
certain decisions pending before the Board of Trustees. Your
letter correctly states that I advised you to disqualify
yourself from participating in these decisions. However, I
wish to clarify that my advice was limited to the requirements
of the Political Reform Act 1/ and did not concern your duties,
if any, under Government Code Section 1090.

The Fair Political Practices Commission currently has no
jurisdiction to interpret or enforce Government Code Section
1090 because that statute is not part of the Political Reform
Act. Insofar as your letter states that I advised you
concerning conflict of interest questions covered by Government
Code Section 1090, your letter is inaccurate.

If you have any further questions concerning this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Staff Counsel

_______________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

KED:jaj

P.O. Box 4014
Richmond, CA 94804

certain decisions pending before the Board of Trustees. Your

of the Political Reform Act 1/ and did not concern your duties,
if any, under Government Code Section 1090.

The Fair Political Practices Commission currently has no
jurisdiction to interpret or enforce Government Code Section
1090 because that statute is not part of the Political Reform
Act. Insofar as your letter states that I advised you
concerning conflict of interest questions covered by Government
Code Section 1090, your letter is inaccurate.

If you have any further questions concerning this matter,

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan

KED:jaj

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

RICHMOND UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT
1108 BISSELL AVENUE
P. O. Box 4014
RICHMOND, CALIFORNIA 94804
Telephone (415) 234-3825

R. W. LOVETTE
SUPERINTENDENT OF SCHOOLS

June 10, 1987


Ms. Eddis Harrison, President
Richmond Unified School District
Board of Trustees
1108 Bissell Avenue
Richmond, CA 94802

Dear Eddis:

After much consultation with Mr. Russ Taylor, our District's legal counsel, and
further discussions with Mr. Paul Loya, school attorney, and Cathy Donovan, a lawyer
with the State's Fair Political Practices Commission, I find that, due to financial
interests, I must disqualify myself from participating in any RUSD Board actions in-
volving the employment of System Architects Engineers, Inc., (SAE).

Ms. Donovan of the FPPC suggested that I declare my position to the Richmond Unified
School District Board and thereby avoid any present or future questions that might
arise around "conflict of interest," as defined in Government Code Sections 1090
through 1097, Section 87100 and other related sections.

I have been assured by the staff of FPPC that this letter of declaration is sufficient
to protect the Board and myself, and does not in any way affect our contractual agree-
ment with SAE.

Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please feel free to
contact me. Thanks.

Cordially,


GEORGE E. CANTU, TRUSTEE
Richmond Unified School District

GEC:bjc

CC: Trustees, Board of Education
Dr. Lovette
Mrs. Cathy Donovan
Mr. Russ Taylor
Mr. Paul Loya

sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission currently has no jurisdiction to interpret or enforce Government Code Section 1090 because that statute is not part of the Political Reform Act. Insofar as your letter states that I advised you concerning conflict of interest questions covered by Government Code Section 1090, your letter is inaccurate.
sections.question_synthetic: Does FPPC jurisdiction extend to conflicts of interest under Government Code Section 1090, and did FPPC staff properly advise a school trustee on disqualification requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC's jurisdiction is limited to the Political Reform Act and does not extend to Government Code Section 1090. The FPPC staff counsel clarifies that prior advice was limited to Political Reform Act requirements only, not Section 1090 conflict of interest duties.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter from school trustee and FPPC's response letter. No formal question/conclusion sections present, but FPPC response letter serves as advice clarifying scope of prior guidance. Included school district letter provides context for the advice inquiry.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does FPPC jurisdiction extend to conflicts of interest under Government Code Section 1090, and did FPPC staff properly advise a school trustee on disqualification requirements?

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission currently has no jurisdiction to interpret or enforce Government Code Section 1090 because that statute is not part of the Political Reform Act. Insofar as your letter states that I advised you concerning conflict of interest questions covered by Government Code Section 1090, your letter is inaccurate.
